title: The One Where Eddie Moves In

jokes.text: Casa de Joey. Huh? I decorated it myself.
jokes.setup: Joey proudly presents his apartment
jokes.punchline: The pretentious 'Casa de Joey' followed by obvious pride in terrible decorating
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey character comedy - his earnest pride in something objectively bad. Well-integrated with character but fairly predictable setup.

jokes.text: Get out. No. / Wow, Joey, this is, uh... / Art. / Art it is.
jokes.setup: Friends trying to be polite about Joey's terrible decorating
jokes.punchline: Ross struggling to find a word, Chandler sarcastically supplying 'Art', Monica's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat comedy with economical dialogue showing each character's personality in their response to awkwardness.

jokes.text: Well, check this out. Is it a coffee table? Is it, uh, a panther? There's no need to decide.
jokes.setup: Joey showing off what should be a normal piece of furniture
jokes.punchline: It's so bizarre it could be either furniture or animal, presented as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with great economy. The 'there's no need to decide' is perfectly Joey - treating confusion as a feature.

jokes.text: So tell me, is this genuine Muppet skin?
jokes.setup: Commenting on what appears to be a fuzzy pillow or fabric
jokes.punchline: Treating it like it's made from actual Muppets with deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler line - absurd concept delivered with his signature deadpan style. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: Hey, excellent water-table thing. / Thanks. I love this. But you know what? It makes me want to pee. / Yeah, me too. I think that's the challenge.
jokes.setup: Ross complimenting what seems to be a water feature
jokes.punchline: Joey admits it makes him need to pee, Chandler treating this as an intentional design challenge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent comment to absurd conclusion. Chandler's 'that's the challenge' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Well, uh, it's because he had a thing with the... With the thing.
jokes.setup: Joey asking why Chandler didn't come to see the apartment
jokes.punchline: Ross's completely non-explanatory explanation that everyone accepts
jokes.explanation: Decent awkward moment showing how the group avoids discussing the Joey-Chandler roommate situation.

jokes.text: Nice toilet. / No, no, no. Behind it. / You have a phone in here? / That's right. I have a phone. In here.
jokes.setup: Joey showing off his bathroom, Monica commenting on the obvious
jokes.punchline: The 'impressive' feature is a bathroom phone, treated as luxurious
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke playing on Joey's misplaced priorities about what's impressive in an apartment.

jokes.text: Joey, promise me something. / Yeah. / Never call me from that phone.
jokes.setup: Monica asking Joey to promise her something important
jokes.punchline: The 'important' thing is not wanting bathroom phone calls
jokes.explanation: Well-structured setup/punchline with good economy. The formal 'promise me' setup makes the mundane punchline funnier.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I claimed you in the name of France four years ago.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she's been 'discovered'
jokes.punchline: Ross treating discovery like colonial exploration
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ross intellectualism turned absurd. Great economy and the 'France' detail makes it perfectly nerdy-Ross.

jokes.text: Ross, if you care about me, get the pie out of the man's hood.
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting excited about her music deal
jokes.punchline: Apparently someone's pie ended up in someone else's hood during celebration
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual that we can only imagine. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What are you doing? / I'm sorry. My pie was in your hood.
jokes.setup: Following up on the pie-in-hood situation
jokes.punchline: Ross having to awkwardly explain/retrieve pie from stranger's clothing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that pays off the absurd setup. Ross's embarrassed politeness makes it work.

jokes.text: I have to get the coffee out of that guy's pants and I'll be back in the hospital by 7.
jokes.setup: Monica in work uniform dealing with celebration chaos
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact statement about removing coffee from stranger's pants as part of her job
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment treating bizarre situation as routine work duty. Monica's matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Calm down. I'm blow-drying. / Blow-drying what? You have no hair.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his long time in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out the logical flaw in his excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with great economy. Monica's direct logic cuts through Ross's excuse perfectly.

jokes.text: Get out, you doofus!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to be mature about the sibling conflict
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverting to childish name-calling
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Monica's inability to stay mature. 'Doofus' is a mild but fitting insult.

jokes.text: Or, you know, he's rubber and you're glue.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to give mature advice about sibling conflict
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggesting playground-level solutions
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan commentary on the childish behavior. Rachel's dry delivery makes the playground reference work.

jokes.text: I've never wanted you more.
jokes.setup: Ross and Monica having an immature sibling fight
jokes.punchline: Ross being turned on by Monica's aggressive behavior (toward Rachel)
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment with Ross being inappropriately aroused by conflict. Economy is excellent - just five words.

jokes.text: You know what they say: Ask your slippers a question... you're going crazy.
jokes.setup: Joey asking his slippers if he should call Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joey making up fake wisdom to justify his behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Joey character moment - creating fake conventional wisdom. The setup/punchline structure works well.

jokes.text: You had a thing. / Yeah, well, I hear the place looks great. / Oh, forget about it. I'm having a ball, ha.
jokes.setup: Joey and Chandler having an awkward phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Both clearly lying about their situations to save face
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven awkwardness showing both trying to maintain pride while clearly missing each other.

jokes.text: Was that the oven timer? / That's right, my friend. / It's time for Baywatch! / Baywatch!
jokes.setup: Chandler hearing a timer sound
jokes.punchline: It's actually Eddie's Baywatch alarm, with Joey enthusiastically joining in
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection showing Joey and Eddie's shared enthusiasm for mindless TV.

jokes.text: Can you believe they gave Stephanie skin cancer? / I can't believe they promoted her to lieutenant.
jokes.setup: Joey concerned about character's cancer diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Eddie more concerned about her job promotion
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Eddie's twisted priorities. Great structure with parallel 'I can't believe' format.

jokes.text: See, this is the brilliance of the show. I say always keep them running. All the time running. Run. Run, Yasmine. Run like the wind.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining what makes Baywatch good
jokes.punchline: His 'analysis' devolves into just wanting to see attractive people run
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - trying to intellectualize his shallow interests. The escalation to 'Run like the wind' is great.

jokes.text: I figured I'd have time alone with my thoughts. But it turns out I don't have as many thoughts as you'd think.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why living alone hasn't worked out
jokes.punchline: His honest admission about his mental limitations
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey character moment with perfect setup/punchline structure. His innocent self-awareness makes it endearing rather than mean.

jokes.text: He's decorated.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining that Joey has moved on from Chandler
jokes.punchline: Treating interior decorating as evidence of emotional recovery
jokes.explanation: Great economy - two words that callback to the apartment tour and suggest Joey's emotional state through decorating.

jokes.text: Oh. Oh, I thought they were just watching me. Like at an aquarium, you know?
jokes.setup: Phoebe discovering she has backup singers
jokes.punchline: She thought they were observers like zoo visitors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - the aquarium comparison is wonderfully weird and fits her character completely.

jokes.text: Honey, we can talk about this. It's just that it's costing about $100 a minute to be in here.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wanting to discuss the emotional reality of her cat song
jokes.punchline: Producer's blunt prioritization of money over artistic integrity
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire showing the clash between artistry and commerce. The specific dollar amount makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Look at the cranial ridge, okay? If Dino was a velociraptor, he'd have eaten the Flintstones.
jokes.setup: Ross getting into a debate about cartoon dinosaur classification
jokes.punchline: His paleontologist expertise applied to cartoon logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ross character moment - applying real science to cartoons with complete seriousness. Very in character.

jokes.text: Did she leave a number? / Did you see me write one down? / I don't have her number, butt-munch.
jokes.setup: Professional phone call situation
jokes.punchline: Eddie's sarcastic response followed by Rachel's childish insult
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation showing Eddie's attitude problem and Rachel's frustration. 'Butt-munch' is juvenile but fits the moment.

jokes.text: Sweetie, before I forget, did I leave my diaphragm at your place? / Hi, Mom.
jokes.setup: Monica answering what she thinks is a work call
jokes.punchline: It's her mother asking an inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline with excellent economy. The timing and Monica's mortified response make it work well.

jokes.text: Is that Joey guy gonna pick up his moose hat or should I toss it?
jokes.setup: Eddie asking about Joey's belongings
jokes.punchline: The specific absurdity of a 'moose hat' as Joey's possession
jokes.explanation: 'Moose hat' is a perfectly Joey item - ridiculous but specific enough to be believable for his character.

jokes.text: At the, uh, supermarket in the, uh, ethnic-food section. I helped him pick out a chorizo.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he met his new roommate
jokes.punchline: The mundane, random nature of bonding over sausage selection
jokes.explanation: Good awkwardness showing how desperate Chandler was for companionship. Chorizo is a funny specific detail.

jokes.text: When'd you start using moose in your hair?
jokes.setup: Joey picking up his moose hat
jokes.punchline: Eddie making a pun about hair product vs. the actual moose hat
jokes.explanation: Decent pun connecting hair mousse with the moose hat. Shows Eddie trying to be clever/likeable.

jokes.text: It'll never last. He's just a rebound roommate.
jokes.setup: Monica commenting on Chandler's new living situation
jokes.punchline: Applying relationship terminology to roommate situations
jokes.explanation: Excellent concept treating roommates like romantic relationships. 'Rebound roommate' is a great coined phrase.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I know. I sound amazing!
jokes.setup: Phoebe hearing her 'Smelly Cat' video for the first time
jokes.punchline: She thinks the obviously dubbed better voice is actually her
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's obliviousness and self-confidence despite the obvious deception.

jokes.text: Pretty, uh, different, huh? / Oh, I am sorry, but I'm incredibly talented.
jokes.setup: Ross diplomatically suggesting something's off about her voice
jokes.punchline: Phoebe completely missing his hint and doubling down on her talent
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Ross trying to be gentle and Phoebe's complete confidence in her abilities.

jokes.text: I do, but, uh, Eddie makes them this way. They're pretty darn good.
jokes.setup: Joey pointing out Chandler's egg preferences have changed
jokes.punchline: Chandler diplomatically defending his new situation while clearly uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Chandler trying to be positive about changes he doesn't really want.

jokes.text: All right, that's it! He just comes in here, Mr. Johnny New Eggs... with his moving the mail and his: 'See you, pals.'
jokes.setup: Joey getting increasingly frustrated with Eddie
jokes.punchline: His dramatic rant about mundane changes, coining 'Mr. Johnny New Eggs'
jokes.explanation: 'Mr. Johnny New Eggs' is a brilliantly absurd nickname. Great escalation showing Joey's jealousy over trivial things.

jokes.text: And now there's no juice! There's no juice for the people who need the juice. I need the juice!
jokes.setup: Joey's rant continuing about household changes
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration about juice shortage with repetitive emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the repetitive 'juice' emphasis. Shows Joey's tendency toward dramatic overreaction.

jokes.text: Nobody likes two kinds of eggs equally. You like one better, and I wanna know which.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming he likes both egg styles equally
jokes.punchline: Joey's insistence that egg preference equality is impossible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - treating egg preferences as a fundamental truth. His earnestness about this trivial thing is great.

jokes.text: You took your eggs, and you left! Did you really expect me to never find new eggs?
jokes.setup: The ongoing egg debate
jokes.punchline: Chandler applying breakup logic to breakfast food
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment treating eggs like a romantic relationship. Perfect Chandler delivery with great emotional logic.

jokes.text: Look, I refuse to get sucked into this weird little Geller dimension thing, okay?
jokes.setup: Rachel watching Ross and Monica's sibling behavior
jokes.punchline: Her assessment of their family dynamic as a separate dimension
jokes.explanation: 'Geller dimension' is a great phrase for their weird family dynamics. Shows Rachel's outside perspective well.

jokes.text: Mon, you've gone ultrasonic again, all right?
jokes.setup: Monica getting very high-pitched when angry
jokes.punchline: Ross's scientific description of her vocal register
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Ross using scientific terminology for Monica's emotional state. 'Ultrasonic' is perfectly nerdy-Ross.

jokes.text: I hated you when we were kids. / You hated me when we were kids? / Yes. I hated you.
jokes.setup: Ross being shocked by Monica's admission
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt confirmation and repetition
jokes.explanation: Good sibling moment with Monica's matter-of-fact honesty about childhood feelings. The repetition emphasizes it well.

jokes.text: I loved you in a you're-my-brother-so-I-have-to way... but basically, I hated your guts.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining her complicated childhood feelings
jokes.punchline: The distinction between obligatory love and actual hatred
jokes.explanation: Great structure showing the complexity of sibling relationships. The 'have-to' love vs. gut hatred is well articulated.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! She's smelly cat!
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizing the voice actress is like an unwanted shelter animal
jokes.punchline: The metaphorical connection to her own song
jokes.explanation: Excellent moment of Phoebe connecting her metaphorical song to real-world injustice. Great character growth moment.

jokes.text: Oh, oh, that song has so many levels.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's realization about the voice actress
jokes.punchline: Her sudden appreciation for her own song's depth
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Phoebe's artistic pretensions while maintaining her genuine care for outcasts.

jokes.text: You like that show? / You don't? / Ha, ha, what...? No. I mean, it's just pretty people running around on the beach.
jokes.setup: Joey and Eddie discovering they disagree about Baywatch
jokes.punchline: Eddie dismissing exactly what Joey said was brilliant about the show
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the roommate incompatibility. Eddie dismissing what Joey praised earlier sets up conflict.

jokes.text: Well, that's the brilliance of it. The pretty people. And the running.
jokes.setup: Eddie dismissing Baywatch as shallow
jokes.punchline: Joey earnestly defending those exact shallow elements
jokes.explanation: Decent callback with Joey maintaining his position. Shows his consistency and Eddie's incompatibility.

tmdb_overview: A record producer discovers Phoebe's 'Smelly Cat' for a music video. Monica resents living with her dating brother again. Chandler has a new roommate; Joey considers moving back.
